reviews.review_text: The wonderful folks at the Jolly Swagman came to our rescue when we really needed a place to stay and we will always be grateful! You won’t find a more friendly, helpful group anywhere who go out of their way to make sure everything is just right. Thank you very much!
Nice friendly staff and park. Close to all the essentials..
2nd stay here, different room so I can't compare but a lovely night's sleep, much needed. Thanks

Great location in the centre of town, clean amenities and super friendly staff! Caravan spots were a little tight but manageable as you tend to spend time in town and not under the awning. Will be back for sunburnt country again next year! Thanks guys!
Small but more than adequate, clean and tidy and looks quite new, friendly reception
Been coming here on and off for several years now,  don't go anywhere else. Dog friendly.
Basic place to sleep.

Stay in our van. Booking was just a phone call, 4 nights over the Christmas period under $200. Close to parks and the pub for meals if we wanted. Nice clean amenities. Not a lot here for kids but a great little find.
The office ladies and grounds staff were very helpful and friendly. Very peaceful spot in the evenings
Good location and staff are really friendly. Caravan sites are close together.
Great al round for a brief stop over in town
Staff was extremely rude

EXTREMELY  RUDE

NEEDED to park my my car near the laundry  for 5min to unload washing  as I HAVE A MEDICAL CONDITION.  Up came a EXTREMELY RUDE MAN. And asked me to move my car due to a man that had a transplant.  I explained i had a  medical condition and I to was lucky to have a transplant. Insulting me. He informed me the transplant man did work. I have had a double transplant for 26 years. I wouldn't INSULT the donor  family,  nor would I damage there  generous gift  to me . LIFE. And this EXTREMELY RUDE man obviously has NO UNDERSTANDING OF LIFE AND BEING NICE. God help the world with people like this.
Friendly service great location

I couldn't believe the size of our room. My laundry is bigger. We had to Jimmy around our bed, this room was definitely not worth $130.
The kettle was that rusty, that we had to go buy a new one. We took a plate out of the cupboard and it had tomato sauce under it. The verandah had carpet on it, which hadn't been swept for a long time. It had rubbish all over it.This is a double room. There was one plate, 1 spoon and no teaspoon. I took photos to show reception, but there wasnt anyone there. For that price, I would have expected some shampoo and conditioner in the shower, at least. Won't be back
Nice, clean and convenient. Good facilities and amenities in the rooms

Fantastic little place to stay. Fantastic late check in arrangements meant that I didn't need to stress about arrival time and I could just concentrate on getting the kms done. Lovely and clean. I will very happily stay there whenever I am travelling through Toowoomba.
Near 2 main roads but quiet of a night.
Very helpful lady ( Kerri) at front desk....thank you.

What a bloody awesome spot.  Friendly and accommodating.  Was great to be able to have our doggo with us in such a lovely park.    Would definitely recommend to any pet owners travel through this area.

We only stayed the night and would have liked to stay longer. We will call again when in Toowoomba. The cabin was very clean and warm. It was raining and very cold outside. |The beds were comfortable and the towels were very clean and soft. |Also very quiet only just hearing sirens.
Customer service is perfect here

This park has a rather short walking distance to Picnic POint where you can enhoy exclelent vuews over greenery forests almost as looking at a green Bondi Beach ! ANd twhere the waterfalls are resembles those in Lions KING! The accomomdation has a gentle athmosphere- countrystyle vibes! And an orange cat who lurks in the bushes ready for a hug ;) It is quiet and protected- I spent time here after a violent attack on me..and it really helped me wine down and breathe again, lower my sholulders <3

Kerri and Brad were some of the nicest and most helpful people I’ve met on my trip around Australia. And that’s saying a lot, as most Australians are super friendly. From checking in, helping me over two days to get the start battery of my car bulk charged and changed a fuse, to casual conversations about life. Gave recommendations for activities in the area, too. Was very pleased with my stay here.

Nice location with clean amenities. Staff is very helpful and pleasant.
Sites are a bit tight but very well set out.
I would definitely stay here again.
Staying in our caravan.  Great location, handy to town.  Sites close but great amenities.   Would stay again.

Great Dark Blue cabin. Only one step up. Carport.
Everything is provided in the kitchen. Queen bed really comfortable. Good position for walking dog.
Lovely lady in office. Very helpful.
We enjoy staying at jolly swagman perfect location clean and tidy place the lady in the office is lovely very helpful.
Management are amazing great location and awesome accommodation.
Great service and excellent customer service
Perfect for the sole traveller. Everything one needs for a night or more.  Air conditioned. A good TV.   Shower excellent. Well looked after tiny room..
Fantastic for the price

Kerry and Brett where great hosts,
The jolly is a lovely park very neat and tidy, everyone was friendly.
It’s in a great location and offers plenty of different room options.
We had a family room it was perfect size for a family of 4.
Will definitely be back.
Great little park, lovely and helpful staff. We had a great stay with our dogs. Recommended for friendliness and price

Stayed in a single room (blue heeler) I think they are called. Room was fairly new and very clean. About the size of fifo mining accommodation. Which was fine as it was the cheapest place in Toowoomba that had aircon and own bathroom. Also had a great convection oven/grill/microwave to reheat food and make open grilled toasties in. Originally booked for 2 nights for work and had to extend by 7 more nights. Bev who was running the park while the current managers were on holiday was amazing at organising my extra week in the same room. Moving bookings around so I could stay where I was. The weekly rate was extremely cheap compared to the rest of Toowoomba.
Amazing
The fit and finish is overdue for a face lift but everything was clean and well presented. Comfy big bed in the Villa. Easy check in. Bev was helpful and on for a bit of banter. Enjoyed our stay.

Came in late, unplanned, to Toowoomba. Called at 2 mins to 6pm which was office closing time. Bev answered & couldn't be MORE helpful and friendly and organised a late check in for a pet friendly bungalow. Neat and tidy and a good feel park. Would stay again.
Great hosts and central location
Good value, surprisingly quiet. Close to a decent pub.
Dust under bed and some crockery not washed

The room was emaculatley clean and I had an amazing night's sleep.  Could not fault the room in any way. The TV backlight had gone but let them know on checkout and staff were so wonderful and friendly. I have stayed in the past and never had an issue ❤️😊
Clean amenities, friendly staff.
Excellent place to stay at a reasonable price the people who run the place are very friendly.
Awesome Little Cabins And Facilities,, Good People ,, and Pet Friendl

On check-in, I was met with a warm and welcoming smile. ||Cabin was clean and refreshing, very inviting. The bed was comfortable. Had the option of a split system to accommodate the day/night temps. Shower has awesome pressure with instant hot water at the ready. Parking directly outside the cabin is limited but there were options given at check-in where I can park. They which were close-by and visible from the cabin in which I stayed in. THANK YOU.
We got a villa it nicely setup and decent size bed and bathroom wouldn't mind staying again

A friend and I stayed at the Jolly Swagman Holiday Park in Toowoomba. |We each had our own single room, the  Heeler cabins. They were clean, comfortable and perfect for our two night stay. The only problem that we found is that they are not non-smoking.  Around us the smokers sat outside and puffed away but unfortunately the smell lingers and drifted into my room as I had the window open.  There was an air-conditioning unit in my room but it was such a lovely afternoon that I didn't want to sit inside, with the door closed and the air-conditioning on. It seemed unfair to me that the smokers got to sit outdoors and enjoy the afternoon while I had to bid away inside to get away from their awful smell.
Neat and tidy park with great service from Kerrie at check in and check out. See you guys next time I'm in town
Tidy clean park in a good location.

We found this place very convenient.
The amenities were nice and clean
Very quiet
Great place to park the Van.
What a dump. Cabins are disgusting. Cockaroachs everywhere. Full of feral permanents. A big no from us.
Great holiday park.  Good facilities, nice pool area with BBQ, games room with couches, TV, pinball machines.  Quiet in residential street.  Some shops around 10min walk. City about 20mins.
Older, clean amenities. Caravan sites are good size
The reception lady on 04/05/2026 were rude. She shouted at a customer who was not sure about the exit gate. My cabin room was out of hot water at night time. Please avoid this place.
An ideal location for our reunion,all services/facilities available for adults & the children.
Very small sites for caravans. Backing in was very tight. Drainage in the showers (men and women) was poor as the cubicles flooded. Maintenance lacking in the men’s bathroom. Not worth $66 fee.

I would have given a 5 star, but after a big drive,we just wanted to have a nice warm shower and cook our dinner but the gas was empty 😔,we were very disappointed with this outcome as it was only 9degrees that night, told the reception lady when I handed the keys back and all she said was ok,no apology or anything, just saying!!,if any person books in make sure you have a full gas bottle for hot showers and for cooking as it is getting colder, thanks
Unhelpful, office closes at 2pm on a Sunday and wouldn't book us in. I have never had a big 4 refuse a booking unless they were full. Not impressed
Everything appears to be quite neglected. The road is unpaved, and all facilities lack maintenance. It feels as though no one is concerned about it.

Do not stay here. We were so disappointed with our stay - cockroaches everywhere, the aircon didn’t work, shower leaked we left early and requested a refund but the management never called us back
Would not recommend to anyone

Dated cabin could use some maintenance.  Small park full of workers so we probably got the remnant cabin.  Laundry average, dryer didn't dry clothes in the time.  Not too far from town.  Staff friendly.   Not very clean or tidy grounds.  Better parks around if I was in Toowoomba again.

We were very disappointed with this park. We stayed here last year and it was fine but this time it looks very untidy and unkept. No cleaning of the amenities for 2 days. No hand towel paper in the ladies toilets. Red mud everywhere. Caravan sites quite tight. Not  happy

We chose this motel as the reviews said that it was convenient for concerts in Queens Park and we were pretty pleased with our choice. The location was great. We stayed in a Budget Double Room which was a bit on the small side but fine (it was basically a portacabin). There was plenty of open parking around the back of the cabin but we noticed that other accomodation on the site had covered parking spaces which would have been much better. It's a bit basic for the price but we would stay there again.

Road is really busy but didnt bother is too much in terms of sleeping. No playground for our 3 year old which was dissapointing. No park near to walk to. Bed was a little hard but sleepable.
Clean and tidy.
Wifi didnt work
No couch to relax on just a table and chairs.
Ok for short stay

Loved this place! This was our first stop on our Brisbane to Perth lap and I’m so happy I chose this instead of a BIG4. The site was spacious, clean, great amenities and staff were lovely. I was freaking out because I was lost and when I called they tried to help me, even though I could barely tell them where I was exactly lol so kind. Would definitely recommend.

Stayed in a Blue Heeler Cabin. Very happy to have found accommodation designed for a single person.

Cabin was small but suited to one person. Includes a desk with chair, wardrobe and kitchenette in the small space! Photos on their website of the cabin were accurate. Had a little trouble with the convection microwave working, but it got there eventually. The manual was provided, which I was grateful for as I hadn't used this type of appliance before.

Air-con was fantastic and the cabin was dark even during the day with the curtain drawn. It was quiet and very easy to sleep.

Pricing was very good value and I ended up staying an extra night as the 7th one was free. It's a great budget holiday with the kitchen to self-cater and multiple supermarkets within walking distance.

Location is good. Tourist information centre is across the road. There's a bus stop a few mins walk away. It's about a 20 min walk into town. There's a small shopping centre about 20 mins walk in the other direction.

Thank you Kerrie and Brad.
Should have hosted  this review at Christmas.
Thank you for looking after us ,as we had major car problems.
You both make us feel welcome.
Thank you both.
And please pass on to your lovely staff.
Colette and Glenn
Neat and comfortable rooms.  Staff is friendly and
Awesome place to stay over in staff super friendly would recommend if staying in Toowoomba

Felt very welcome. Managers very friendly and could not do enough to make you comfortable. Had a unit that was next to a beautiful maintained garden, which also made it peaceful and private. Highly recommend, close to CBD, hostpital and easy to get to most areas.
Great place, great amenities, tho a microwave in kitchen would be appreciated.
Clean and tidy little cabin. Comfy beds.
Great location and friendly staff
Great park, plenty of walking tracks across the road. Walking distance to Dan Murphy and pub. We were in time for the beautiful flower displays around town.
Very handy to town & venues. Lovely staff. Clean facilities. Pleasant gardens to walk around. Mixture of sites, suiting large or small vehicles.
Owners are great with service
It is centrally located to Supermarket...  Queen Park ... water bird habitat n the city centre all is in walking distance. I stayed in their Blue Heeler room in all I need.
Excellent service and value for money . Stayed during the Toowoomba carnival of flowers
Simply excellent, in every way. Thankyou to all the staff at Jolly Swagman.

Its in a good location to get around although the area seems a bit dodge and the small interaction with staff was good but I find it strange that this is under the big 4 banner.
The roads are just gravel so kids cant scooter or blade around which really affected outside time. The facilities are old and need a makeover they feel pretty gross. Pool needs some shade and some play space for kids. I booked because it was big 4 and assumed it would have pool slides and activities but its very basic. Even the 8yo was not keen to come back. It could be great with a little love...

No toilet paper in room and we didnt realise until after hours so had to drive to 24hr fuel station in the middle of the night to pay $13 for four rolls of toilet paper. It wouldnt of been so bad if it was just myself but i had my 3 daughters and wife staying. Pretty pethetic if u ask me

Do not stay here. We were forced to wait until 4:00 pm to check in due to “cleaning”, despite an advertised 2:00 pm check-in, and the cabin was still filthy on arrival.

There was a bug infestation, dirty furniture and fridge, and hair and toothpaste left in the bathroom sink. No amenities were provided, and only one bed was made, with no linen for the others.

A nearby cabin left in the middle of the night with their children due to a cockroach infestation, which was extremely concerning. There are also free-roaming dogs throughout the park, with dog waste left around the grounds and not cleaned up.

The office closed at 5:00 pm, and we had to call the emergency number multiple times before getting help. After repeatedly requesting the cabin be cleaned, we were eventually offered a partial refund, which speaks for itself.

Staff were rude and dismissive, and the property was not as advertised. The lack of hygiene, service, and basic standards makes this a place to avoid.

Photos are: bathroom floor, cord of hair dryer, a baby wipe after wiping the floor in the living space, stained couch.

The location is great. All the main things to do and see are within 15 minutes. It is an old park with old facilities so don't expect anything fancy. But for the price we were happy with our pet friendly ensuite cabin. It had everything we needed and was clean enough. The only negatives I have (which I don't blame the park for it just is how it is) were that the park is obviously majority permanent tenants. Not a big deal in general but it did mean that the many kids who live there were in the pool all day every day we were there. It's not that I expected it to myself but it's a small pool and 6-8 kids all with inflatable pool toys takes up the whole pool. All the sun loungers were taken the whole day by the same people. The common room (kitchen, TV etc) had a bunch of the older ones on their computers taking up the whole space and everywhere we went it was like every head snapped to us all at once like we were intruding being anywhere. I felt uncomfortable/unwelcome most of the time I was on the grounds. The cabin across from us was permanently tenanted and had lots of flashing Christmas lights on all night which illuminated our cabin (they are about 3m apart). It wasn't the worst but I definitely wish I had been able to enjoy the place a bit more. If you just want somewhere cost effective to rest your head after you're out exploring most of the time then I recommend. If you're looking for somewhere to relax more often than I wouldn't.

Zero communication even though we confirmed late check in after driving 1300 kms.
Strangers walking past gave us the code to get in after waiting at gate and making call after call. Messages unanswered, phone calls unanswered. Another stranger called the son of the owner to get code for safe. Don't recommend at all.
Not an ideal environment for children or family holiday.

Probably the most dated and run down Big4 ive stayed at around the country however the staff were lovely and rooms were clean , as long as you dont expect updated park facilities and cabins like most other big4 parks its ok for a night or 2 .

Not a BIG4. This was an Australian version of an American trailer park. Not a holiday park, its a permanent residency park. Really felt like we needed to lock EVERYTHING up, not relaxing, kids who reside here permanently just walk right through your camp and take over the pool. Tried to have relaxing swim, but kept getting hit with a large solid ball and splashed around! Also pool is not very refreshing, its in full sun and NO shade! Really did NOT enjoy our time here! Also, not suitable for tents. Seen tents were allocated concrete slabs in full sun!!

This Park is well appointed, clean and tidy. The staff are very helpful and happy. We stay for 4 nights. The pool and BBQ area is well maintained. |Just 10min from the city centre. Local shops just 5min walk to get you last minute milk bread etc. we would not hesitate to recommend this place for your next holiday. Cheaper than the average Motel and definitely value for money. We will use this facility |Next time we go to Toowoomba.

We stayed for a while, and the woman who started was incredibly kind, but those who replaced her were much less welcoming (except for one young woman). A lot of food was stolen from the refrigerators. I'm not accusing the establishment in any way, but they did absolutely nothing to access the security cameras to find the thief. They often ask when you plan to leave, which makes you feel like you're in the way. Otherwise, the place is amazing; I just hope you get the young woman at reception, and keep in mind that the camera in the common room is completely useless.

I have stayed here twice now, when I brought the kids it was amazing for their pool and playground and jumping pillows. The place itself is also it a quiet area. Reception was always helpful and very friendly. However both times I’ve stayed the cabins are always filthy, cups that haven’t been washed, linen with hair all over it, the toilet seat was sticky with hair and yellow substance and the shower not much better, even with a “we’ve serviced your room card” the same day.

Would be a good place to stay if there was a little love and cleanliness behind it.
Had a caravan powered site, great pool, good pool area for kids. Playground and jumping balloon.

Great place to stay. Staff friendly and helpful. Rooms were clean and perfect with everything you need. Bed was a dream. After not having 2 good nights sleep was great to lay down and have a good night sleep.

The Park is run down.
I had a terrible experience with managers at front desk - not friendly, Not welcoming what’s so ever! 0/5 rating.
I should've listened to the reviews. This place is run down and management are snarky and rude. Do not reconmend

An extremely packed park. In the cabin section you are only allowed a car. Trailers, boats etc must be parked out on the street which to most would be unacceptable. The staff are polite and friendly, except one who must have been having a bad day however they really did know the rules, yep THE RULES. Finding any alternative may be difficult.

Very basic but adequate for a short stay. Our villa had undercover parking set in a beautiful garden setting. All utensils and TV made for a comfortable pet friendly stay. A small settee would have been nice however..
Clean and suitable cabin. Out dated but provides you with all the essentials you need. Prime location to walk to gardens and coffee shops etc. Well managed park and very tidy.
Only stayed the one night and it was a great little cabin and it was pet friendly 🐕🐕
Good location right in the city. Close to everything.
Very clean spacey cabins, friendly people, definitely stay here next time thru
Very friendly owner's, lovely and clean place.
Modular room at a cheap price , clean and tidy . Fine when you just Neeta place to sleep .
A great  caravan park in the middle of town. Good clean amenities regularly serviced.
Great park, incredible people and service. Thanks for everything guys!

We stay here frequently when traveling to Toowoomba, and the park managers are always lovely and helpful. The cabins are clean, comfortable, and very accommodating, and the playground is a big hit with the kids. I would definitely recommend this park to everyone.

One suggestion: During our stay, there was heavy rain, and the gutters were quite blocked with plants growing in them. This caused a loud noise throughout the night, which made it hard to sleep on those nights.
New ownership, lack of communication and follow up. May improve with time…

Do not book here - our stay was 1-6 Oct 25. It is the worst maintained park we have ever stayed in, and should not be a BIG4.  We should’ve looked at Reviews before booking. I gave only 1 star because of generally quiet location, proximity to shops and Toowoomba city, and pleasant interactions with office and cleaning staff.|1. Communication. Days prior to arrival leaving phone and website messages to extend booking went unanswered, until we phoned the AH Emergency number to night manager so our request would be actioned. A return call the next day finally gave an apology with an explanation that the park was recently sold as the excuse for poor staff handling. We hoped that this would be the only negative for our stay , but this was not to be . |2. Sale of property. If true, then the previous owner has withheld not completing badly needed maintenance. These are:|a. Women’s showers - badly dripping outdated old-style shower roses wasting valuable water, a light sensor which does not turn on in quite dim conditions limiting shower use ,( when the men’s light always turns on when entering!), I noticed a shower wall with a sticker from a swimsuit/underwear liner that a lazy foul patron has just stuck it on a wall, but the cleaning staff have not removed and has been there the whole 5 day stayover. The bathroom smells like it needs a good dose of bleach down the drains. Which maintenance installer would think to put soap dispensers and hand dryers at 1.6 m high? No common sense. Where children cannot reach and water drips down your arm while using dryer. More modern eco shower roses would save water wastage.|Surely even if patrons have not complained or reported the dripping  roses and light faults the cleaning staff should have. So a response  on day 3 saying that repairs would have to wait for the maintenance staff is an excuse to send me on my way, when this already should’ve been identified, reported and a maintenance person allocated to the job. |b. Small sites. We have a 8m motor home and a car. While the site was fine for our motorhome- just!; the car had to be parked across front of concrete area , as the parking as per the site map should’ve been between our concrete path and the next van, but this was too close to open car doors without impacting our side curtain.|c. The internet only works in the camp kitchen area. Hot spotting doesn’t work either. Very poor connectivity and likely the cheapest plan. So the advertised Free Wifi is useless and misleading.|d. The roads in the far east of the park have potholes so big and wide and deep - in deplorable condition requiring urgent repairing. |e. No yellow bin for recycling. Very poor and disappointing not to have this available. |In general this park is in poor run down old condition requiring significant repairs and maintenance,  and funds to improve it. We didn’t even bother to try pool, BBQ or camp kitchen. Whilst this is in a quiet location, I would not come again nor advise anyone else to either. Bathroom cleanliness and maintenance is our most important item in a caravan park. Sadly, this park is way off the mark which does nothing to attract repeat customers.

Do not book here - our stay was 1-6 Oct 25. It is the worst maintained park we have ever stayed in, and should not be a BIG4.  We should’ve looked at Reviews before booking. I gave only 1 star because of generally quiet location, proximity to shops and Toowoomba city, and pleasant interactions with office and cleaning staff.
1. Communication. Days prior to arrival leaving phone and website messages to extend booking went unanswered, until we phoned the AH Emergency number to night manager so our request would be actioned. A return call the next day finally gave an apology with an explanation that the park was recently sold as the excuse for poor staff handling. We hoped that this would be the only negative for our stay , but this was not to be .
2. Sale of property. If true, then the previous owner has withheld not completing badly needed maintenance. These are:
a. Women’s showers - badly dripping outdated old-style shower roses wasting valuable water, a light sensor which does not turn on in quite dim conditions limiting shower use ,( when the men’s light always turns on when entering!), I noticed a shower wall with a sticker from a swimsuit/underwear liner that a lazy foul patron has just stuck it on a wall, but the cleaning staff have not removed and has been there the whole 5 day stayover. The bathroom smells like it needs a good dose of bleach down the drains. Which maintenance installer would think to put soap dispensers and hand dryers at 1.6 m high? No common sense. Where children cannot reach and water drips down your arm while using dryer. More modern eco shower roses would save water wastage.
Surely even if patrons have not complained or reported the dripping  roses and light faults the cleaning staff should have. So a response  on day 3 saying that repairs would have to wait for the maintenance staff is an excuse to send me on my way, when this already should’ve been identified, reported and a maintenance person allocated to the job.
b. Small sites. We have a 8m motor home and a car. While the site was fine for our motorhome- just!; the car had to be parked across front of concrete area , as the parking as per the site map should’ve been between our concrete path and the next van, but this was too close to open car doors without impacting our side curtain.
c. The internet only works in the camp kitchen area. Hot spotting doesn’t work either. Very poor connectivity and likely the cheapest plan. So the advertised Free Wifi is useless and misleading.
d. The roads in the far east of the park have potholes so big and wide and deep - in deplorable condition requiring urgent repairing.
e. No yellow bin for recycling. Very poor and disappointing not to have this available.
In general this park is in poor run down old condition requiring significant repairs and maintenance,  and funds to improve it. We didn’t even bother to try pool, BBQ or camp kitchen. Whilst this is in a quiet location, I would not come again nor advise anyone else to either. Bathroom cleanliness and maintenance is our most important item in a caravan park. Sadly, this park is way off the mark which does nothing to attract repeat customers.
Friends of ours stayed there.It was very suitable for what they needed

We had a cabin for one night.||Being school holidays it was good value for money.||Good kitchen, large fridge, sofa, table and 6 chairs, TV, Air-conditioning, microwave, oven, gas stove top 4 burner and usual kitchen appliances.||We had 2 bedrooms, 1 x Queen Bed and second room had 2 x Bunks.||Good size bathroom, toilet and Shower.||Parking by the door.||Balcony with table and 2 chairs.||We didn't get to look around the park but saw on the map that there was a good size camp kitchen, laundary and pool. ||Powered, unpowered and ensuit sites. Lots of cabins. ||Wifi didn't work at cabin and none on map said it worked best in the camp kitchen.||Boom gates.||A little distance from the CBD but very quiet.

We booked here as it was the closest dog friendly site to the flower carnival. Considering it’s a small site in the middle of a suburb, it’s relatively quiet and we had no issues with the permanent residents.|Yes the site is small, but has all the facilities you need, including local shops in walking distance. The camp is clean and tidy, albeit dated and in need of some investment and TLC. The staff work hard to try and maintain it. The ensuite site (92) was large enough for the big vans and you didn’t feel crammed in like other commercial sites. There was even a fifth wheel on site, so even though it’s small it’s accessible to bigger vans. I think it would be wise to call ahead if there’s any questions regarding accessibility etc. Overall we enjoyed our stay and would recommend. I only have it 3 stars based on how dated it is, but otherwise it’s a great little site.

We booked here as it was the closest dog friendly site to the flower carnival. Considering it’s a small site in the middle of a suburb, it’s relatively quiet and we had no issues with the permanent residents.
Yes the site is small, but has all the facilities you need, including local shops in walking distance. The camp is clean and tidy, albeit dated and in need of some investment and TLC. The staff work hard to try and maintain it. The ensuite site (92) was large enough for the big vans and you didn’t feel crammed in like other commercial sites. There was even a fifth wheel on site, so even though it’s small it’s accessible to bigger vans. I think it would be wise to call ahead if there’s any questions regarding accessibility etc. Overall we enjoyed our stay and would recommend. I only have it 3 stars based on how dated it is, but otherwise it’s a great little site.
Mold in the air conditioner.  The entire cabin smelled of mold. The shower curtain had mold along the lower edge.

Very disappointed and frustrated with big4 Toowoomba. I had booked for one night worth $165. And the following day they called
me that they have to cancel my booking because there’s has been a missed up or say double booking and they will refund my money back. But I’m waiting for nearly 4weeks now and still haven’t got my refund. I have been contacted with the big4 but they told me there is a new owner and they insist that they already been refunded me but there’s no way I got my moneyback. I even send them my bank statement to let them know that my money its not there after they insist that its already been refunded. And I really hope this could be resolved because its not acceptable of taking my money into my
Account and saying that I will get a refund and its not there.

Stayed in an ensuite camping spot. The area was weird, grass wasn't flat, sort of grass but had stones in it, there was a drain and taps right in the middle and the next caravan waste hose in it, and I needed a ladder to plus in power as the point was on the roof facia... Dumbest spot ever stayed in. Plus had a nice view of 3 bins, an old pallet, shed roller door, and the road.
The ensuite was a fun trip back to the 80's! brick wall, shower curtain that blows on your legs, low pressure, no exhaust fan and  a window that didn't open, it was just like your grandparents shack up the river! I thought Mike and Carol Brady were going to visit... lol... Not good if your looking for any sort of luxury.
Had a cabin during flower festival. Not busy here at all. Good cabin. Easy to get to cbd. Loved watching the group of cats over the fence.
Staff were very nice and location was good. Dog friendly. The WiFi internet is terrible and doesn't really make it to the sites.

Basic room, clean, could do with a bigger fridge, not the bar size that was in it, as we struggled  for space. Another  towel rack would have been handy also. Quite  location,  camp kitchen  was good, pool also looked inviting.

This has to be the most run down big 4 park I've been to, my cabin had roaches on the ceiling and talk about having roaches in a small cabin lucky it was only for a night, staff were great a shame I had to leave this review

Absolutely loved staying here with the kids..staff are all kind and attentive the cabins are a delight plenty of room for the whole family and plenty of fun for the whole family. I will definitely be coming back

My husband and I stayed in a cabin overnight. It was spacious, clean and had a very comfortable bed and lounge, with a large kitchen and full size fridge. Parking was at the door and the park was quiet and safe and the tariff very reasonable. We would definitely stay there again.
Quiet area close to basic shops, but quite a distance from anything. Basic.

I would NOT recommend this place. I contacted them days before arriving to say that we were going to be arriving after hours and I requested that the after-hours check in info be emailed to me instead of text, as I was coming from NZ and did not have an Australian phone number (their policy states that a SMS message will be sent so you must have an Australian number).
I heard nothing from my first email, so I emailed them again the day we were due to arrive. I received a reply from someone in the office stating that my after-hours check in would be no problem and that the info would be sent to me vis SMS at 4pm that day.
I never received anything from them, despite providing them with my my email address and NZ phone number. By the time we landed in Brisbane it was 10pm. I tried to phone them, but no one answered. So there we were, two adults and three children, stranded at Brisbane airport, with no way of accessing our accommodation in Toowoomba. We ended up having to pay for a night's accommodation in Brisbane and travelled to Toowoomba the following day. I reached out to them twice following our stay to express my dissatisfaction at their lack of communication and requesting a refund of the first night's accommodation, however no one ever responded to my emails. Extremely poor service and far too expensive for the very basic facilities they provide. Do not waste your time with this place.

So disappointed in this caravan Park. It shouldn't be a Big 4. Way too expensive for a small (clean) cabin, paid $175 for the night at Big 4 special rates! Should have been half that cost as the cabin is situated amongst messy permanently occupied vans. Some they have put fences around to hide the mess of the residents. Also funny how I didn't get the usual email asking me to review my stay!!
Friendly staff and helpful, they helped me out with caravan site (until my car was fixed) for extended stay, clean and good amenities, also thank you Daryl for moving my van.
Was ok. Bit hard going to toilets thru everyone else's camp site though..

This is a small park with so many things packed into it. The staff were very friendly and assisted us with an overflow site. Pool, spar, games room and camp kitchen (even after pizza oven). Amenities were clean and moden. Great spot.

We stayed here with a caravan and an extra person in a swag. The caravan site we were given was quite small and tight, making it a bit challenging to maneuver. It was fine for just one night, though. One thing we didn’t like was having to use a key for the amenities. As a family of three adults, we only had one key, which was a bit inconvenient.

Unfortunately, the toilets hadn’t been cleaned during our stay, and the showers were a bit of a hassle. While they were nice and hot, the water sprayed every time you moved, which meant my clothes kept getting wet even though they were on the bench.

On a positive note, it was pet-friendly, which was a big plus for us.

However with all that in mind we still had a pleasant stay.
Clean but older style amenities. Poor to non existent WIFI
Really enjoyed our stay close to town centre the 3 kids were never bored plenty of fun stuff to do at the park will return for future stayz

Super friendly staff. Cabin was great, well equipped and really comfy.  We had a great stay. Kids loved the go carts and jumping pillow. Grounds were tidy and it was super quite. No idiots partying which was nice.
Would recommend for sure. 👍

Terrible. The place is extremely run down, initially evident in the shredded Big 4 flag flying out the front, and run down bathroom facilities. We did not feel safe here, and the power kept dropping out. The drive through site that we were given made it impossible for the neighbouring caravanner from getting their car and van out, luckily we were only there one night. Roads were extremely narrow, making it hard to exit. The lady in the office was lovely, just a shame Big 4 don't invest in the upkeep of the park as we normally pick Big4 as they are typically high quality parks. Photos on the website are misleading.

A nice little caravan park in a quiet back street but access is tricky because the road is sometimes congested and there isn’t any check in parking space.
The van sites are tight and getting a van onto a site can be tricky.
The park is said to be suitable for Big Rigs, I would disagree.
The facilities are well maintained if somewhat dated
There is a pool and a jumping mat for the kids .
It’s close to a small shopping centre and about 15 minutes from the CBD
Great park. Great service and extremely helpful

I will generally choose a Big 4 park to stay at where available but, on this occasion I regret it!
The sites are narrow, we had to get the neighbour to move his car so we could get out of our space.
The facilities are very dated. The shower had very low water pressure taking longer to rinse the shampoo out.
The soap and hand dryer are so high up it's really difficult if you're short in stature to reach.
Staff were pleasant but the park over all was a let down!
Looks like a great service. I tried to send an email to you via your website but your server is down. As an international booker, how can I get in touch with you besides your landline?

Caravan sites for 3 nights so no comment on cabins. While the park and amenities were clean, it seemed very cramped. Amenities were small and cramped, caravan sites very close together making getting in very tricky. Very tight corners on exit route for caravans. Camp kitchen was excellent, however inadequate signage. Staff hard to access.

The property is very dated and not worthy of a Big 4 tag. |Really annoyed when the pool is advertised as heated but in fact was freezing as was the spa. The whole park lost power for a couple of hours on our first but we were not given any information.

Big shout out to Matt my main maintenance man! You go above and beyond the call of duty. Helped me out on more than one occasion and also witnessed you helping other campers during our 6 week stay with your cool, calm demeanour. Love the sox too😉
Very friendly staff.Exceptional service. The Shower was awesome 👌 Clean with great surroundings.
Room 45. Very very smelly room. Almost unbearable
Very friendly helpful staff.  They were very helpful when I needed it for extending   Park is tidy.  Good location. Nice grassy sites.  Dog friendly.

The place was really nice the staff where lovely although the rooms where on the small side and someone came into our room uninvited when we were in bed and the door was banging open in the thunderstorm
It was raining but it was still a Lovely place

Poor standard for a Big4, overpriced small cabin.  Good camp kitchen but with no toilets.  Key needed for toilets.  Why, when you have to use a token to enter the park?   It's not like you have people wandering into the park.

Sarah was amazing. After a rough night camping in the rain and wind causing a lot of water inside our tent, We decided to ask for a cabin instead for the remaining stay and Sarah made everything so easy which after a long night in the elements made everything seem better. Can’t thank you enough

We are having a 10 yr reunion motorcycle National Rally here and  there have been a lot of upgrades. Staff have been excellent and supported our Worlds Greatest Shave event and Rally dinner held in the indoor camp kitchen I suppose. We are a large group motorcycles and welcomed by Big4 Toowoomba
All in all I think we all will be back in another 10 years if STill able.
Amazing place to stay at was there for 3 weeks for work great service can't really complain
All big 4 staff were amazing, cabins were clean and tidy on arrival. With a good size pool and a playground for kids, I look forward to coming back to stay again.
Kev and his wife and staff were amazing very helpful and friendly hope to be back soon 😀
My family and I stayed there about 3 weeks ago for one night.. and I will never go back.

This place was perfect for our needs. We booked the 6 bed cabin and, though tiny, was still a comfortable fit for the weekend.
The park amenities were great. The kids loved the jumping pillow and go karts. The pool was a bit cold so we didn’t really use.
The whole park was kept really clean, felt really safe and the staff were friendly fly when needed.
The check in / out process was really simple too.

Teenagers fighting at midnight, through something at our caravan waking up my 1 year old son, terrifying my wife. Police ended up getting involved.
Will never stay there again.
Rooms are spotless customer service was also excellent. The ground men's take pride in they work..Smiley crowd 💯+
$55 for an ensuite site with power and water is a good price. Friendly staff and good pool on them hot days

Would not recommend this park
Came to Toowoomba for horse races was told on arrival at park they were cancelled, went out for tea and saw races on tv
Very hard to get in and out of sites
Filthy ensuite and overpriced
Very disappointed
Very accommodating fitted us in with caravan for overnight stay. Lovely people running the park.

Overall the stay was ok.  Helpful lady in the office and close to local shops, but we had an ensuite site that needed a refurb compared to most park we have stayed in.   Also the sites are very crammed in. It was also very rule driven with notices everywhere which were not pleasantly written.  The pool area needs some shade and lounge chairs were not clean. No ice on site and no kiosk.
Nice clean caravan park. We stayed on an ensuite site, which was very tight to get our 18ft caravan in. The ensuite was clean and tidy but for a 'Big 4' it was dated.

Sehr freundliches Personal und ein sehr schöner Spielplatz, Pool und Trampolin. Uns hat die Campküche gefallen die sauber  groß und komplett innen gelegen ist gut gefallen.  (Praktisch, da es schlechtes Wetter hatte als wir hier waren)
Punktabzug gibt es für die Bäder, es gab weder Seife, Handtücher noch Toilettenbürsten.....und sauber ist auch anders. Ist man von den BIG4 eigentlich anders gewöhnt

Staff at the park were great.  A stuff up with keys not being left out for our late arrival was sorted out soon enough with an apology and a smile.
The onsuite sites are rather dated but clean and serviceable.  Sites are not particularly large, but fine for an overnight stay which is all we needed.
Mix of short and long term sites does give it a bit of a "trailer park" feel - so maybe not the place for a longer holiday stay.
One of the worst parks you could stay at, in Queensland. Possibly Australia.

Arrived at Big 4 caravan park, people were very welcoming upon arrival. My father had a nice room for the same price we recieved an underwhelming room. Our rooms were incredibly different considering the price. My young daughter was locked inside of the bathroom due to a faulty door latch, the fan in our main bedroom was either barely moving or carrying on like cyclone Katrina that was going to take the roof off the joint. We also had the same issue in the living area. The couch provided had holes throughout and looked like it was second hand. We then had people move into the accomodation next to us, there outside area was all but in our bedroom and could hear every word they were saying. Tried to call the reception to get us moved but the line was constantly unavailable with no after hours phone number provided. Our room was also filled with multiple bugs and my partner felt uncomfortable with the surrounding people consistently. The photos provided on the website definitely make this place look better then what it is.

So I booked over the phone for a drive through site and asked if they were busy as I wanted to possibly stay an extra night. The lady on the phone was really nice and said that it should be fine.

Upon arrival we noticed how tight the place was with a larger caravan. You can't drive your caravan in the driveway for check-in. You have to park on the street. It's a busy suburban area and parking was a slight hassle. Once checking in I was told that Someone was on the drive through site that we had booked. They then said they could still put me on a powered site. That was fine so I asked if we could stay an extra night. Was then told the entire place was booked out all week. I'm glad that we didn't stay an extra night though. It was extremely tight to get a 21ft caravan out of the exit. We managed but I reckon there would of been a foot of space on one of the turns.

Unfortunately, I can't really recommend this place. It's like the spot that the Big 4 business forgot about. I think Big 4 bought an aging caravan park and then just never updated it.

We stayed overnight in a campervan site with ensuite. The ensuite was very clean and practical, a bonus if you don't fancy a walk to the amenities and prefer your own privacy. The campervan sites are a little tired looking and lack visual appeal but are nonetheless practical and quiet as they are situated far enough away from the main hub of activity.
The receptionist was very friendly, welcoming and helpful and we enjoyed our overnight stay.

My daughter and I stayed her on a powered site overnight the lady’s where amazing and went above and beyond for us and we got an upgrade
To an ensuite suite was a massive help as we were staying in a roof top tent.
Highly recommend staying here

Cabin was roomy and clean. However no soaps or other toiletries supplied. Only 1 bathmat for a 3 bed cabin. The amenities were good, pool and BBQ area clean and tidy. Jumping pillow a win with the kids. Main bed was like sleeping on a rock, but some people like a firm bed.
The mangers kicked us out cause they cancelled my booking not happy with the new mangers

Hi everyone my experience of big 4 Toowoomba well it wasn’t what I expected I was put in a cabin at the exit to the park where people try to get around the gate my car was parked in the correct park I had multiple times of motorcycles going around the gate I was lucky my car wasn’t damaged so I asked could I have another cabin and they said we will upgrade you , the next cabin had a lingering smell of cooking it was thu the curtains bed linen and even the pillows and aircon it made me sick 🤢 I couldn’t even sleep that nite it was putrid kind regards Paul
Stay on a powered sited, great location friendly & helpful owners and staff
The staff were very sweet, upgraded us for free. Clean rooms, lovely people.

Vendors and patrons that come to
Town for the Murphys Creek Chilli Carnival love staying here!! 😎 it’s only 20 minutes to Murphys creek grounds also!
The overall experience of our stay, was very good, nice and quiet with friendly staff.
Clean and tidy . Bit expensive. Nice staff
Tight spots for caravans..  .heard sirens day and night
Cabins have what you need, great spot for kids
good spot for en-suit camping. good access to all the parks and hiking.
Very friendly and helpfull staff, enjoyed our stay, will definitely come back.

It was a last-minute booking during peak time, so my cabin choices were limited.  My bedding configuration was fixed without any issues by staff upon arrival because of my booking mistake.
Cabin #89 was spotless, and the double bed looked new, it also had twin bunks.
The cleanliness of the cabins is a major reason why I would definitely stay here again in a larger cabin.
Although it's not a flashy new park, it's located in a great location and features a large pool, communal room, BBQ station,  Pedal carts and jumping pillow.

Great holiday park close to CBD! Lots of accomodation to choose from at reasonable prices. Grounds and facilities clean and tidy. Units are close together so not much privacy but "neighbours" were friendly and quiet. All staff were friendly and helpful too. We arrived an hour before check-in  and would have been happy just sit around the beautiful pool area but we were able to check-in early as our unit was ready for us!!
A lovely park with amazingly accommodating friendly staff. Super clean cabin (well done Michelle). Close to everything but so very quiet at night. Will definitely stay here again.👍😊

Bad first impressions,stepping into dog poo,hot water in ensuite didn't always work.
Pool was damaged in places and safety certificate on fence expired. My daughter and my husband ended with an ear infection from the pool.
On the plus side it's close to everything and when we first arrived to find toilet leaking,they came straight away for us to swap enquires.

I have just recently stayed at the Big4 Toowoomba. Due to overnight storms, some electrical issues had evolved in my cabin.. I was upgraded, with a few more issues in my next cabin residence. Can I add the Managers and Caretakers at the time were extremely helpful, prompt, and gracious in getting everything under control. I will take away a respectful and thoughtful manner in which everything was dealt with. A fantastic, family friendly place to be.

Me ha gustado mucho, tenemos ya 3 días y todo ha salido muy bien, la gente muy amable y me gusta porque tiene piscina, parque para niños y alquilan unos buggys muy divertidos para niños mayores de 7 años en adelante.

Wow what a wonderful stay we had. Our son got married on 4th Nov and spent a week at Big 4. The wedding guests also stayed eight cabins in total. The staff were amazing in organising us to be close together. We held a BBQ for 35 in the facilities, with ease and each cabin was perfect in every way.
Thank you Big 4 Toowoomba all our friends have said you are the place to stay when in Toowoomba. They will be back! AND SO WILL WE. 🙂
Location is very convenient.  Staff are extremely friendly & helpful.

Many thanks to Janita who handled my booking last Friday. Although I rang back several time to adjust the booking, she was very professional, kind and handled my booking with a can do attitude. I have owned several  customer focussed businesses and acknowledge when I’ve had 5 star service. The park itself was tidy with good access and facilities were spotless.

Very dated and overcrowded. We were advised by text the day before we arrived to park on the street outside the park. Then check in but leave any trailer on the street outside! This is largely due to the park being overcrowded with cabins. We had a very small tradie trailer on the back of the car full of valuable tools, and due to the extremely high crime rate in the location, we were not comfortable leaving it  overnight on a public street.
I feel these instruction should have been made clear at the time of booking… not the day before arriving. Had we known this, I would have booked somewhere else!
The cabin was very basic and in dire need of renovation. This park needs to demolish some of these old outdated cabins to provide better parking facilities for guests … it’s a very poorly planned park!
Will not stay here again … overcrowded and overpriced!
Good facilities bloody expensive to just pitch a tent.
Cabins are on the small size, grounds and facilities well kept and clean. Great location.

We arrived lovely clean cabin read in cabin information there was a vending machine in laundry went to laundry no vending machine so proceeded to office to ask about said machine and was rudely told in laundry asked if there was only one laundry and again rudely spoken to phone rang so dismissed me completely so l left sol think the lady in the office on Saturday 30 September needs to go for a walk around park and find vending machine.

We stayed in a wonderful cabin, very well cleaned and with everything we needed! The staff were all very friendly and made us feel very welcome!
The facilities were clean and the garden well maintained. The pool was great and they even put a new jungle gym for the kids!
I highly recommend this caravan park to anyone visiting Toowoomba!

This school holidays I decided instead of going out of town (as im a local) i would book a weeks stay at the big 4 toowoomba and omg wow wat an awesome experience its been! From a petting farm to kids club to the awesome play centre and pool its been amazing! My children have had an absolute ball!! This was definitely a win for us. The cabin we  were in was spotlessly clean everything available from a kitchenette to reverse cycle air conditioning lots of space surrounded by beautiful gardens! Also love how there were  various different fruit trees available to pick lovely juicy fruit from. The managers were so warm and welcoming we will definitely be back what a great experience its been! Thank you big 4 toowoomba and the team cant wait to return 😀

I have just spent 5 days at The Big 4 on Eiser st Toowoomba and I loved it.
I had 3 of my Grandkids with me school holidays and as you say happy kids happy heart they loved everything available to them at this Park.
Their was kids club everyday where Staff interacted with the kids for 2 hrs a day, there was an animal farm on the Friday where their were pony rides and so many animals for the kids to play with. Alpacas goats ducks chicken Guinea pigs lambs etc it was just beautifully organised and so much fun. The Owners are just gorgeous and Represent this Holiday Park with pride as they deserve. Toilets Showers and Rooms are very clean and fresh on a daily basis, the Pool is so inviting the pretty gardens all over the Park are beautiful  so It’s a yes yes yes for me I’ll be back and if your looking for great accomodation The Big 4 on Eiser St Toowoomba is by far the best stay for Your Family .. thanks guys I loved it.

We loved our stay here, the staff was very kind and helpful. The grounds are very well kept as are all the facilities. And the cabins are lovely and spotless, 5 stars to the housekeeping team! 😉
Will definitely come back next time we are in the area!

A beautiful tidy little caravan park free of main road traffic noise. Sarah the receptionist really helped us out at 10 to 5pm with a spur of the moment holiday change of plans. Cabin was neat and tidy with great facilities ..lovely little park will stay again
Warm regards
Becky

Lovely lady at the front desk could not be more helpful if she tried very welcoming( Kimberly )“The cleanliness of the room and right down to the cute bow made on the toilet paper roll” the grounds are kept very nice and nice and quite at night great location and will be back next time though

I had to cancel a booking made for a powered caravan site at this park due to a medical appointment with a specialist in Brisbane. I contacted the park numerous time to cancel. An employee told me she would look into a full refund due to my appointment. They gave me $15 back out of $45 and I contacted them 4weeks prior to our stay.
Nice place to stay
Nice clean, tidy and quiet location 👌

Will never stay again charged us $4.20 in eftpos charges was given a cabin that had no balcony was very basic no side lamps no room to hang anything in bathroom the towel
Rail fell off & the room was $140 per night we can stay at lovely motels for less or a little more the staff where rude told my husband it was complimentary & not part of the service when we asked housekeeping for fresh towels after 2 nights of our 3 day booking very disappointed in this park having stayed in many van parks & never had any of these issues.

Nice clean , tidy and secure
Park.
Great clean and tidy ..close to shopping center
We stayed in a cabin. It suited our needs. It was clean and comfortable. We were happy with the room and would recommend it.

A disappointing facility with old style facilities and old fashioned power access... power access looks a bit dodgy. Narrow roadways making parking bigger vans very difficult especially when long term patrons are allowed to double park work trucks further narrowing roads. Dump ponit is by children's play area. We had to park in narrow street more than 50 metres from park entrance to check in as this suburban street is the designated check in area but no short term parking allocation on road.
Good option

If booking here don't expect any sympathy if you get very ill. Want to just move booking back to next year but was too bad from management and to cancel booking. Every other park I had to move helped me out. Easy money grab. Won't be back after several nice stays.
Clean!!!

Nice park, amenities aged but clean & tidy
Fast food outlets nearby
this stinky old dump needs bulldozing

Staying for 3 nights as my husband needed medical treatment. The young lady in reception was very friendly and helpful when we booked in. The first night I had the best nights sleep I have had in a long time.
Cabins are old and could use a little bit of tlc, but very clean with everything you need.
Would definitely recommend and stay again if needed.
Thank you 😊
We really enjoyed our stay. We had a great site with easy access to the amenities.  The were fantastic and very friendly and helpful.  Highly recommend your park.
Nice caravan park.  Enjoyable stay.  Recommend a visit and stay.

Neat and tidy Caravan Park that's got older but clean amenities. Pet friendly. A lot of permanent. Front of desk was very friendly. There is a pre check in before you get there which is good which means you get to your site or cabin quicker. Good value at $40 a night on March 30, 2026.

Spent 2 nights here in our 22” van.
An older park but the grounds are very clean and tidy.
The sites are a generous size and we had plenty of room to reverse in.
Good TV and Optus reception.
There is a nice walking path at the bottom end of the park that goes around the lake.
A negative for me was the height of the keypads for the entry and exit boom gate. They are low to the ground and difficult to reach from a 4WD , especially when towing.
Overall we enjoyed our stay and would definitely stop here again.
Very well presented caravan park.  Spacious sites and close to shops.

Stayed here in our caravan. Yes the facilities are a little old with low pressure but they are clean. The staff were amazing and facilitated our staying and rebooking twice while remaining on our site. Good location and no issues in or with the park. Thank you 🙏

I had an unfortunate experience with management during my recent stay.

I was advised that visitors were allowed as long as they parked in the designated visitor car park, so I had a few friends come by during my stay based on that understanding.

However, I was later approached and told I was not allowed to do so, as it was considered “conducting business,” which had not been clearly explained to me beforehand.

I attempted to clarify the situation via email to ensure I was following the correct rules, but was then stopped at checkout and questioned about it, which felt quite uncomfortable and unnecessary.

I fully respect that parks have their own policies, but clearer communication from the beginning would have avoided this situation entirely.
Over priced no WiFi old fersilitys open to parkland were anyone can walk in they have  gone cashless  and you need to carry a phone to do washing

Had booked from Dubai to stay there tonight. A non-refundable Cabin booking. However, am unable to leave Dubai. Whilst there was no obligation, I received a full refund. Very rare but a lovely gesture in this day and age. cheers
Was there twice las year thats why we came back.it is now $10 extra. And they don't except cash only cards . So will not be back .

Cabin ist sehr in die Jahre gekommen.
Es gibt kein free Wifi / kein Internet.
Man kann nicht draußen sitzen.
Great park. Older style cabins but very clean and tidy. No issues here
A good central spot for sight seeing
Great place to base a trip to check out Toowoomba or it's events.
The staff are amazing friendly. The accommodation is great. We have stayed in a few of the cabins each time they have been clean and very accessible.

Stayed at a powered site.
Pleasant caravan park, in the centre of town.
Amenities block is a bit dated, but other then that no complaints.
Would stay here again

We have stayed at Toowoomba Motor Village a few times and enjoy returning.  The sites are a generous size, grassy but not shaded.  Amenities are clean, dated but still functional.  The camp kitchen is spacious and has everything needed for a barbecue.  Staff were very helpful, cost is reasonable with the GDay discount. Recommend.

Excellent location, everything you need within walking distance.
Friendly staff! Clean amenities. Dog friendly!
A Beautiful place to stay with a walking track right on the back of the property. Highly recommend.

We stayed six nights in our caravan on a powered site. The staff were friendly and helpful. The site was plenty large enough for our caravan, its awning and our tow vehicle. The site was opposite a nice grassy reserve. The site had a concrete slab for our van and the  awning, though they were sloped. The amenities were clean and in working order, though they need a renovation. The park was very quiet. There is only one boom gate for entry and exit and the keypad for the gate was a little difficult to reach from our tow vehicle. On the whole, I would recommend this caravan park.

Decently priced powered site close to kitchen, laundry and amenities. Very friendly and helpful managers. Good atmosphere overall. There's a service station out front and takeaway food nearby. It's about a 10-15 minute walk to nearest supermarket. Unfortunately no pool, but Toowoomba's elevation seems to provide cooler weather and a nice breeze. Ended up staying a second night. Plenty to do in town. Would recommend staying here.

Terrible air conditioning unit s they are set on low and will not go higher it was 36 degrees and I  had my snow dog with me and specifically asked for cabin and air conditioner paided extra for the comfort after working my ass off all year all I wanted was to chill relax and recharge after a big yr. Instead I stayed outside under a tree till 8.30 pm because it was cooler than the cabin I could have camped in my tent and Been more comfortable. Floors were dirty, bed extremely uncomfortable. Cooker not working had a dirty rusted element on top of gas stove top. Manger didn’t care about my comfort even when I told him I’ve traveled 4hrs, really disappointed when this is what it’s all about especially at Christmas .
Nice park with clean rooms and good facilities.
Great spot, clean rooms that are well appointed and good value. |Plenty of amenities close by. Easy and friendly check in
Great location Budget friendly

Karla and her husband here at Toowoomba caravan Park are the kindest and most welcoming owners. Were extremely helpful in giving us some linen for a overnight stay for our new van that we got caught out on our return trip home. The facilities here are amazing and great place to camp for the night. You won't be disappointed if you stay here!

Before  the Ambulance even left with my husband, the Park's Owners/Managers  came to let me know that they were holding the site for me for a few more days until I knew more about my husbands condition. Opening the windows during the day helped with this. The outsourced bed linen was a lovely addition which a lot of accomodation providers at this price don't opt for and it does make a big difference! The bed was comfortable and the full kitchen, although dated (but very clean) had everything needed for my length of stay. My two nitpicks aside from the above: the lack of wifi. Even at a budget accomodation price point, until this stay, I’ve never not had this service available. Lastly, more a point for my room in particular, 104. Having the housekeepings dirty laundry bins right by the front door is not the best first impression and creates a lot of activity by the front door during their working hours (not great when the bathroom windows are right next to this, doesn’t give you any privacy). In my opinion, there was plenty of space in staff only areas where these could be kept. Overall, I enjoyed my stay and would return again.

There was no point for us to drive to Toowoomba simply to turn around and drive back so we decided not to go and sought to get our money back from Toowoomba motor village. They point blank refused.
